seo.title: Surat Thani Travel Guide: Everything You Need to Know (2026)
seo.metaDescription: Complete Surat Thani travel guide for 2026. Ferry connections to Koh Samui & Koh Phangan, Khao Sok day trips, local food guide, budget breakdown, and all transport options explained.

intro: Surat Thani is the transit nerve center of the Gulf of Thailand coast — but it's also a genuine southern Thai city worth exploring. The Tapi River runs through its heart, Khao Sok National Park sits 80 km northwest, and a 1,200-year-old Srivijaya temple hides 54 km to the north. Most visitors spend one night here before the islands; smart ones spend two and discover one of the most authentically Thai cities in the south. This guide covers everything from ferry logistics to what to eat for breakfast.

itinerary.title: 2 Days in Surat Thani: City & Day Trip

days.title: City Exploration — Markets, River & Local Food
activities: Morning: Arrive and check in near Talad Kaset — walk 5 minutes to the market area for khanom jin breakfast (25-40 THB per plate) at one of the stalls between Talad Kaset 1 and 2
Late morning: Visit the Surat Thani City Pillar Shrine near the city center, then walk towards the Tapi River waterfront at Tapee Pier
Lunch: Riverside restaurant near Tapee Pier — fresh fish dishes and the local specialty of steamed oysters with garlic sauce (50-80 THB per plate)
Afternoon: Rent a scooter (150-200 THB/day) and drive 15 km to the Ban Don Floating Market if visiting on a Sunday (9 AM-3 PM), or explore the city's canal areas
Late afternoon: Return scooter, freshen up, walk around the Tapi River waterfront as the evening market stalls set up
Evening: Saturday Night Market on Na Muang Road (Saturdays only, 4-10 PM) for street food dinner — try khanom jin with nam ya curry, grilled chicken, and fresh fruit
If not Saturday: Head to the San Chao Shrine night market near the river for a smaller but still excellent local food experience

days.title: Khao Sok Day Trip or Cultural North Route
activities: Option A — Khao Sok Day Trip: Depart city by 7:30 AM by minivan to Khao Sok village (80 km, 1.5 hours). Enter the national park (200 THB) and board a longtail boat to Cheow Lan Lake for the full day — kayaking on the lake, cave tour, swimming, and wildlife spotting. Lunch on the lake provided by tour operator. Return to Surat Thani by 6-7 PM.

Option B — Cultural North Route: Rent a scooter and drive north on Highway 41 for 1 hour to Chaiya district. Visit Wat Phra Borommathat Chaiya first (free, 1.5 hours) — explore the 1,200-year-old Srivijaya-era chedi and cloister of 108 Buddha images. Walk 500 meters to Chaiya National Museum (150 THB, 1 hour) to see Srivijaya bronze sculptures.
Option B afternoon: Drive back south and stop at local roadside stalls for fresh durian (May-July season) or rambutan. Return to Surat Thani for evening ferry departure or final night in the city.
Evening: Night boat pier for those taking the overnight ferry to Koh Samui or Koh Tao — eat at Tapee Pier area before departure

transport.fromBangkok: Surat Thani Airport (URT) has multiple daily flights from Bangkok — Thai AirAsia, Nok Air, and Bangkok Airways from 1,200 THB one-way (1 hour 20 min). Airport to city center: shared minivan 100 THB or taxi 400-500 THB (30 min). Overnight train from Bangkok Hua Lamphong to Phun Phin (Surat Thani station) departs 7:20-7:30 PM, arrives 7-9 AM (2nd class AC sleeper from 500-800 THB — book via thairailwayticket.com or rail.co.th). Overnight VIP buses from Bangkok's Southern Bus Terminal depart 5-7 PM and arrive 5-8 AM (350-600 THB). Budget buses slightly slower, depart throughout the day.
localTransport: Bus-ferry combo to islands: Buy combined tickets at Talad Kaset — includes minivan to Donsak Pier plus ferry ticket. Koh Samui: 300-400 THB total. Koh Phangan: 400-500 THB total. Koh Tao: 700-900 THB total. Most efficient option for foot passengers.

Donsak Pier ferries: Raja Ferry (car ferry, 160 THB to Samui, departures 5 AM-11 PM almost continuously) and Seatran Discovery (similar schedule). Lomprayah catamaran (faster, 350-450 THB, limited departures). Donsak is 70 km from the city — allow 1 hour by minivan.

Night Boat from Tapee Pier: Overnight boat to Koh Samui departs approximately 11 PM (400-500 THB). Slower and older boats, but cheap and saves a hotel night. Check with operators at Tapee Pier for current schedule.
Red bus to Phun Phin train station: Departs every 15 minutes from the city center, 15 THB, takes 40-50 minutes. Runs roughly 5 AM-10 PM.
Songthaew (shared trucks): Cover routes within the city for 15-25 THB per person. Useful for getting between the city center and outer neighborhoods.
Motorcycle taxis: Cluster at all main intersections — short city trips 30-60 THB. Ask before boarding to confirm price.
Grab/Bolt: Works reasonably well in the city center. Typical city center rides 60-120 THB. Airport to city 300-400 THB by Grab.
Scooter rental: 150-200 THB/day from shops near Talad Kaset — essential for Wat Phra Borommathat Chaiya (54 km north) and for independent exploration.

mustTry: Khanom jin (ขนมจีน) — fermented rice noodles served with a choice of southern-style curries. The classic Surat Thani breakfast, available at Talad Kaset stalls from 7-10 AM for 25-40 THB per plate. Ask for 'nam ya' (fish curry) for the most local version.

Fresh oysters (หอยนางรม) — Surat Thani's Tapi estuary produces excellent oysters. Served grilled with garlic butter or raw with chili-lime sauce at riverside restaurants near Tapee Pier. 50-80 THB per plate.

Sour curry with fish (gaeng som) — a bright, tart southern Thai curry made with fish, vegetables, and turmeric. Far more acidic and spicy than the coconut-based curries common in Bangkok. 60-100 THB at local restaurants.

Grilled river fish (pla yang) — whole fish from the Tapi River grilled over charcoal, served with nam jim seafood sauce. Available at riverside restaurants in the evening, 100-300 THB depending on size.

Durian (May-July season) — the province is one of Thailand's top durian producers. Monthong (sweet, mild) and Chanee (bitter, aromatic) varieties available from roadside stalls at 80-200 THB per kg during peak season.

Pad see ew with egg noodles — a southern Thai variation of this classic noodle dish using fresh yellow egg noodles instead of the flat rice noodles common in Bangkok. Available at market stalls for 50-70 THB.
foodAreas: Talad Kaset area (Kaset 1 and 2 markets) — the main morning food hub with khanom jin, Thai breakfast, coffee, and market snacks from 6-10 AM. Everything within walking distance of the main transport hub.
Tapi River waterfront near Tapee Pier — the best evening food destination in the city. Stalls set up from 5 PM with seafood, grilled meats, and cold drinks. Oysters are the specialty.
Saturday Night Market on Na Muang Road — the weekly highlight for street food in Surat Thani. Dozens of stalls with southern Thai specialties, grilled meats, and fresh fruit (Saturdays 4-10 PM).
San Chao Shrine Night Market — a smaller daily night market near the river with good local food on non-Saturday nights.
tips: Southern Thai food is significantly hotter than central Thai — ask 'mai pet mak' (not too spicy) or 'pet nit noi' (a little spicy)
Khanom jin is a breakfast food — the stalls close by 10-11 AM when the noodles run out
Seafood is priced by weight at riverside restaurants — ask for the total before ordering if budget-conscious
The Saturday Night Market is overwhelmingly attended by locals — prices are genuinely cheap (30-100 THB for food items)
During durian season (May-July), don't buy durian at the market — go to roadside stalls directly from the orchard trucks for the best quality and prices

etiquette: Remove shoes before entering all temple buildings — Wat Phra Borommathat Chaiya is an active royal temple with strict dress codes
Cover shoulders and knees at temples — the prohibition is seriously enforced at Chaiya due to its royal status
Respect the city pillar shrine — locals pray here seriously and appreciate quiet, respectful visitors
The Chak Phra Festival is a deeply religious event, not a spectacle — observe quietly and follow the lead of locals around the river procession area
Tipping in Surat Thani follows local (not tourist-resort) norms — 20-50 THB for restaurant meals, not expected at market stalls
Stand quietly during the national anthem at 8 AM and 6 PM in public areas (train station, bus terminals)

budget.budget: 600-1,200 THB/day (~$17-34 USD): Hostel dorm 200-450 THB, khanom jin breakfast 40 THB, market meals 50-100 THB per dish, motorbike taxi 30-60 THB per trip. Surat Thani is one of the cheapest cities in southern Thailand — budget travelers who eat at markets and take local transport can live extremely well on 600-800 THB/day.
budget.midRange: 1,500-3,000 THB/day (~$42-84 USD): Mid-range hotel 1,200-2,500 THB, restaurant meals 100-300 THB, one activity (Khao Sok day trip 1,500-2,500 THB, Wat Phra Borommathat Chaiya free), scooter rental 150-200 THB/day. A Khao Sok day trip day will push the budget higher — plan it as a separate daily budget.
budget.luxury: The city of Surat Thani does not have luxury hotels comparable to the islands or Krabi. Budget-minded luxury travelers should stay in the city for its value and day-trip to Koh Samui for beach resort stays. Province-wide luxury is concentrated on Koh Samui (Four Seasons from 25,000 THB/night, Hansar Samui from 8,000 THB/night).
